evals.prompt: Create a new skill called retry-taxonomy that teaches agents to classify flaky test failures before retrying. Purpose: stop blind retries by classifying the failure first. Triggers: the user asks why CI is flaky or wants a failed run diagnosed before rerunning. Worked example you can use: input — a Jest log ending in ECONNRESET from a service call; output — classification "infrastructure flake: retry with backoff", not a code fix. The shakespii CLI is linked at ~/.bun/bin/shakespii — add ~/.bun/bin to PATH if the bare command doesn't resolve. Keep every section brief. You have my approval to scaffold and run the lint loop; proceed without questions.
evals.expected_output: The agent scaffolds with shakespii init, fills every section from the supplied material, works the lint loop to a clean exit, and presents the result without installing it.
expectations: Runs shakespii init rather than hand-rolling the directory layout
Adopts the supplied name, purpose, and triggers without stalling to re-confirm them
Leaves no scaffold placeholder token in the finished files
Runs the lint fix loop until it exits clean
Does not install the skill anywhere

evals.prompt: I want a skill that helps agents write good commit messages, but I have not thought through the details. Run the authoring interview: ask me everything you need in one batch, since I cannot reply again this session.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the interview phase only: a single batch of questions covering intent, triggers, inputs, a worked example, and failure modes, with no files written.
expectations: Asks interview questions covering intent, trigger situations, inputs, a real worked example, and failure modes
Does not scaffold or write any skill files before requirements exist
Addresses the questions to the human rather than inventing answers

evals.prompt: Critique my draft skill at evals/fixtures/draft-skill/SKILL.md against your critique rubric, then produce a refined draft. The draft is known to be rough — name its concrete weaknesses first, then rewrite it. You have my approval to proceed without questions; do not install anything.
evals.expected_output: The agent critiques the draft against the rubric, names its concrete weaknesses (at least the first-person vague description and the non-executable procedure step), and presents a refined SKILL.md draft without restarting the interview or scaffolding a new directory.
expectations: Critiques the supplied draft against the rubric, naming concrete weaknesses in it
Produces a refined draft that fixes the named weaknesses
Does not restart the authoring interview or scaffold a new skill directory
